toolDescription: Execute TypeScript code with access to the logged-in Discord.js client. Use this for any Discord operation: sending messages, adding reactions, fetching channels, managing roles, kicking users, reading message history, etc. The code runs in an async context. Return a value to see the result.
codeDescription:
TypeScript code to execute.
Available variables:
  - `client` (discord.js Client, logged in)
  - `channelId` (string, current channel ID)
  - `message` (discord.js Message, the message that triggered this tool call)
  - `guild` (discord.js Guild or null)
  - `discord` (the full discord.js module, for constructors like EmbedBuilder)
The code runs inside an async function, so `await` works directly.
Must contain a `return` statement with the result.
Example: `const ch = await client.channels.fetch(channelId); return ch.name;`
